content.problemStatement:
Guddu likes a girl that loves playing with numbers. She agreed to go on a date with Guddu, but only if he can solve the following problem:

An integer is round if it is greater than 000 and the sum of its digits in decimal representation is a multiple of 101010. Find the NNN-th smallest round integer.

However, Guddu does not like playing with numbers at all and he is unable to solve this problem, so he begs you to solve it for him. In return, he will definitely give you a treat.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the NNN-th round integer. It is guaranteed that this number is smaller than 2642^{64}264.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The smallest round integer is 191919 and the second smallest is 282828.

solution.timeComplexity: The number of digits of n is O(log⁡ n), so calculating the sum of digits requires O(log ⁡n) time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) No extra space is used.
